title: SplitFaceFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a split face feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setAlongVectorSplitType
methods.description: Sets the split type to project the splitting tool along the direction defined by the specified entity.

methods.name: setClosestPointSplitType
methods.description: Sets the split type to be a curve that defined by projecting the splitting curve to the closest point on the surface.

methods.name: setSurfaceIntersectionSplitType
methods.description: Set the split type to be a surface to surface intersection. If the split tool is a curve it will be extruded into a surface to use in the split. If it's a surface, the surface will be used and optionally extended to fully intersect the faces to be split.

properties.name: facesToSplit
properties.description: Gets and sets the faces to be split. The collection can contain one or more faces selected from solid and/or open bodies.

properties.name: isSplittingToolExtended
properties.description: Gets and sets whether or not the splittingTool is to be automatically extended (if possible) so as to completely intersect the facesToSplit.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: splittingTool
properties.description: Gets and sets the entity(s) that define the splitting tool(s). The splitting tool can be a single entity or an ObjectCollection containing solid and/or open bodies, construction planes, faces, or sketch curves that partially or fully intersect the faces that are being split.

properties.name: splitType
properties.description: Returns the type of split type currently defined.

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

related_samples.name: Split Face Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split face feature.

classType.title: SplitFaceFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setAlongVectorSplitType.title: SplitFaceFeatureInput.setAlongVectorSplitType Method
setAlongVectorSplitType.description: Sets the split type to project the splitting tool along the direction defined by the specified entity.
setAlongVectorSplitType.return_description: Returns true is setting the split type was successful.

parameters.name: directionEntity
parameters.description: An entity that defines the direction of projection of the splitting tool. This can be a linear BRepEdge, SketchLine, ConstructionLine, or a planar face where the face normal is used.

related_samples.name: Split Face Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split face feature.

setClosestPointSplitType.title: SplitFaceFeatureInput.setClosestPointSplitType Method
setClosestPointSplitType.description: Sets the split type to be a curve that defined by projecting the splitting curve to the closest point on the surface.
setClosestPointSplitType.return_description: Returns true is setting the split type was successful.

related_samples.name: Split Face Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split face feature.

setSurfaceIntersectionSplitType.title: SplitFaceFeatureInput.setSurfaceIntersectionSplitType Method
setSurfaceIntersectionSplitType.description: Set the split type to be a surface to surface intersection. If the split tool is a curve it will be extruded into a surface to use in the split. If it's a surface, the surface will be used and optionally extended to fully intersect the faces to be split.
setSurfaceIntersectionSplitType.return_description: Returns true is setting the split type was successful.

parameters.name: isSplittingToolExtended
parameters.description: Specifies if the splitting tool should be extended so that is fully intersects the faces to be split.

facesToSplit.title: SplitFaceFeatureInput.facesToSplit Property
facesToSplit.description: Gets and sets the faces to be split. The collection can contain one or more faces selected from solid and/or open bodies.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitFaceFeatureInput_var.facesToSplit

# Set the value of the property.
splitFaceFeatureInput_var.facesToSplit = propertyValue
